text: Gracie I saw something in the Post-Standard about that eviction court program at Keystone. They quoted a staff attorney — was that you?
That was Daniel. They called me first but I was in the middle of a filing deadline. Classic.
He did a fine job. Though I always think you explain things better. You get that from your father.
Dad barely finishes a sentence, Grandma.
I mean his heart. The explaining is all yours.
I'll take it. Talk soon — Maya's practice starts in 20 and I'm still at the office.
Hey, any chance you can grab Maya from Corcoran fields at 5:30? Daniel just dropped an emergency eviction brief on my desk, due tomorrow morning. I can make it work if not but it'd help.
Of course. Does she need dinner after? I'm making that pasta she likes.
She'd probably love that. Tell Lorraine I'll have her home by 8. And thank you — seriously.
Lorraine already texted me, she knew you'd be buried. That woman has your schedule memorized. 😄
That tracks. She's been doing it since my 2L year. I owe her about a thousand favors at this point.
We're keeping count. Go file your brief, honey.
Thinking of driving up Saturday, maybe bringing Emma. Lunch on Salina, that pasta place you liked? Does that work with your schedule?
Yoga's done by 10:30 so yeah. Keep it to lunch though — I've got a Keystone brief due Monday and it cannot become a whole afternoon.
Quick in and out, promise. Can we pop by to see Grandma Ruth after? She keeps asking when you're coming.
Yes. I'll call her Friday night so she's not caught off guard. And tell Emma to actually confirm before I block my calendar.
She's confirmed — shift trade went through. You mentioned a housing case last call, anything new on that?
Still in intake, can't say much. See you Saturday. Text when you hit Onondaga County and I'll have coffee ready.
